steps.title: Find the known side
steps.content:
From the figure, $AC=28$. The problem says $AB$ is $11$ less than $AC$, so
$$AB=28-11=17$$

steps.title: Use the altitude triangles
steps.content: Since $BD$ is perpendicular to $AC$, triangle $BDC$ is similar to triangle $ABC$: they share angle $C$, and each has a right angle. Corresponding sides give a direct ratio.

steps.title: Match corresponding sides
steps.content:
The hypotenuse-to-leg ratio in the smaller triangle matches the same ratio in the original triangle.
$$\frac{BC}{BD}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{28}{17}$$
The answer is 28/17.
